questions.question: You are flying a 35 DME arc clockwise from the 332° radial to the 079° radial of the VOR. How much distance will you travel on the arc?

To avoid Class B airspace, you decide to fly an arc around one of the airspace layers to stay clear. You are going to arc on the 16 DME arc and go from radial 165° to 012° counterclockwise. How many nautical miles did you arc?
Assuming calm wind, how long does it take to fly a 12 DME arc counterclockwise from the 133° radial to the 048° radial at 94 KTAS?

You are instructed to hold east on the 090° radial at 31 km with right turns and standard legs. While abeam the holding fix on the outbound leg, you center a second CDI needle and determine you are now on the 081° radial at 31 km. With this information, what is the radius of your outbound turn?

During a DME arc for your instrument checkride you decide you want to impress your examiner. He tells you to take the 7 DME arc north for 35° to intercept the 075° inbound course to the ILS. Ground speed is 94 KTS. The ILS portion takes 5.5 minutes. How much longer will you be before landing?
At 25 DME, how many radials are crossed in 1 NM flying perpendicular to a VOR?
What is the speed of an aircraft flying perpendicular to a VOR if it is 4.5 NM away and the bearing pointer changes 12° in 31 seconds?
You are orbiting a fire at 3 NM and 12,000'. If you make 9 full circles around the fire, how many nautical miles did you travel?
Reference the previous question. How far would you have traveled if you flew an arc between the radials at that DME instead of your course?
questions.context: Referenced question 20: While on a cross country you have been keeping track of your progress using VOR radials. If it takes you 5 minutes to cross 17 radials and your ground speed is 119 knots, how far away is the VOR?

questions.question: You join a 35 DME arc at the 040 radial while flying 236 KIAS. You are flying at 8,000' (ISA -7°C), and the altimeter setting is 29.67". Assuming calm winds, how long will it take to fly 15° of the arc?
Orbiting around Mount Adams at 9.2 SM, how many nautical miles do you cover per orbit?

You are flying a holding pattern at 18 DME, and you notice the CDI deflected 4° off the selected course while you are abeam the holding fix on the outbound leg. Assuming no wind, what is your turn radius in the holding pattern?

While practicing DME arcs with your instructor, you fly inbound on the 120 radial, then turn right to arc Northeast on the 14 DME arc. If you fly on the arc for exactly 14 minutes, and your TAS is 124 knots, what radial are you crossing when you exit the arc? The wind is calm.
Refer to the previous question. If you had GPS in your airplane and were able to fly directly from the entry point of the DME arc to the exit point, how much time would you save?
questions.context: Referenced question 22: While practicing DME arcs with your instructor, you fly inbound on the 120 radial, then turn right to arc Northeast on the 14 DME arc. If you fly on the arc for exactly 14 minutes, and your TAS is 124 knots, what radial are you crossing when you exit the arc? The wind is calm.

questions.question: You are testing your new VOR/DME receiver, and you plan to depart the VOR on the 100 radial and fly to 25 DME. Reaching 25 DME, you will arc North and fly the 060 radial back to the VOR. If the winds are calm, and you are cruising at 118 KTAS, how long does it take to complete the test?
How many radials has an aircraft crossed while arcing east if the initial radial was 144° at 12 DME and the distance traveled on the arc is 4.8 NM?

While flying a cross country you decide to make ground speed checks by timing how long it takes to fly between radials. If you maintain 21 DME and cross the 137 radial at 1523z and the 177 radial at 1531z what is your ground speed?
You are inbound on the 147° radial to JVL VOR. You are to intercept and fly the 13 NM DME arc south and west. How far have you flown on the arc if you exit at the 237° radial?
How long will it take you to fly that distance at a ground speed of 174 kilometers per hour?
questions.context: Referenced question 4: You are inbound on the 147° radial to JVL VOR. You are to intercept and fly the 13 NM DME arc south and west. How far have you flown on the arc if you exit at the 237° radial?

questions.question: While flying along the only cloud in the sky happens to be right on your course. The cloud is 5 nm in diameter and you plan to use your excellent depth perception to fly around the cloud at a constant minimum legal cloud clearance distance (that's 2,000' in this particular airspace) and rejoin your original course 180° later on the other side. How far will you fly from the point you start deviating until you rejoin your course?
(Using the information from the previous question) If you are flying at 105 kts how much longer will it take to fly around the cloud than to fly your planned course?
questions.context: Referenced question 15: While flying along the only cloud in the sky happens to be right on your course. The cloud is 5 nm in diameter and you plan to use your excellent depth perception to fly around the cloud at a constant minimum legal cloud clearance distance (that's 2,000' in this particular airspace) and rejoin your original course 180° later on the other side. How far will you fly from the point you start deviating until you rejoin your course?

questions.question: The VOR/DME approach to runway 15 at Martin State (KMTN) airport starts on a 14.7 DME arc with a descent from an altitude of 1,800' at R-039 to 22' at R-060 right over the runway end. If you plan to fly the arc at 90 kts, what is the descent angle assuming a constant descent from R-039 to R-060 (assume calm winds)?
Referring to the previous question: The minimum descent altitude for the approach is 920'. On what radial of the DME arc should you arrive at 920' if you maintain the constant descent to the runway?
questions.context: Referenced question 11: The VOR/DME approach to runway 15 at Martin State (KMTN) airport starts on a 14.7 DME arc with a descent from an altitude of 1,800' at R-039 to 22' at R-060 right over the runway end. If you plan to fly the arc at 90 kts, what is the descent angle assuming a constant descent from R-039 to R-060 (assume calm winds)?

questions.question: You are inbound on the 273° radial to CZI VOR. You are to fly the 20 DME arc northbound. How far have you flown on the arc if you exit at the 345° radial?
As you approach the airport you fly a 12 NM arc counter-clockwise from the 150° radial to the 040° radial. How far will you travel on the arc?
You fly clockwise between the 063° to 137° radials on a 24 NM DME arc around the CVE VOR. What distance did you cover on that portion of the arc?

An aircraft flies east on a DME arc between two VOR radials, 330° and 065°, maintaining a constant 13 nm from the VOR. What is the total distance from starting the arc until crossing the VOR if the aircraft flies inbound on the 065° radial after the arc?
If this were an arc, how long would a full circle be around the NDB?
questions.context: Use the following information for questions 5-7. You are flying between NDB bearings. You cross the 269° bearing at 11:48:42 and then cross the 279° bearing 11:50:54. Your distance to the station is 37 kilometers.

questions.question: You just traveled 19.2 NM while fl ying counterclockwise on a DME arc. You intercepted the arc at the 030° radial and left it upon reaching the 340° radial. How far from the VOR were you while on the arc?
What was the aircraft's MH when you had traveled 10 NM on the arc? Assume calm wind.
questions.context: Referenced question 3: You just traveled 19.2 NM while fl ying counterclockwise on a DME arc. You intercepted the arc at the 030° radial and left it upon reaching the 340° radial. How far from the VOR were you while on the arc?

questions.question: You are 22 NM from a VOR. With a groundspeed 200 km/hr, how long does it take to travel clockwise from radial 090° to radial 120°?
If the time between two radials clockwise, 320° and 015°, is 4:37 and the distance to the VOR is 12 NM, what is the groundspeed between the radials?

An aircraft is flying inbound to the ECK VORTAC on the 186° radial. The pilot intercepts the 27 DME arc and flies clockwise. How far has the aircraft traveled on the arc if he departs it at the 310° radial?
